channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: iPhone and Samsung flashlight tricks you should know
item.description: Your iPhone and Samsung phone flashlights have hidden controls for brightness, beam width and voice commands that most people have never tried.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: You don’t need an SSN to open a credit card: Scammers know that
item.description: More credit card issuers now approve applicants without a Social Security number, giving scammers new ways to commit identity theft using stolen data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to think about Apple Business
item.description: Apple Business is aimed at small businesses coalesced around Macs, iPhones, and iPads. If that’s you, and all your systems are made by Apple, the service is likely to be all you need to run a small operation of up to a few dozen seats.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 20 tricks for more efficient Android messaging
item.description: No matter what type of Android phone you carry or how you usually use it, one thing is a near-universal constant: You’re gonna spend a ton of time messing with messages. The messages may be from clients, colleagues, or your cousin Crissy from Cleveland (damn it, Crissy!).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google should share search data to break its monopoly, European Commission suggests
item.description: The European Commission this week requested, but did not order Google to allow third party search engines in Europe access to its search data as a means to comply with the Digital Markets Act (DMA), legislation the Commission describes as a law designed to “make the markets in the digital sector fai
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New FBI warning reveals phishing attacks hitting private chats
item.description: A joint CISA and FBI warning reveals Russian-linked cyber actors are phishing messaging app users to bypass encryption and access private conversations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to create your own custom Android air gesture
item.description: Psst: Come close. I’ve got something to share with you, and I don’t want everyone around here to hear it. Oh — hi! Sorry, I didn’t realize you were here.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GitHub adds Stacked PRs to speed complex code reviews
item.description: AI-aided development tools are churning out more lines of code than ever, presenting a challenge for reviewers who must review ever larger pull requests.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Phone-free restaurants are trending across the US
item.description: Phone-free restaurants are growing across the U.S., with bars and eateries asking diners to put devices away. Gen Z is driving the shift, per a 2025 survey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DNS security is often inadequate, and network engineers should get more involved
item.description: Despite widespread adoption of defensive measures, most IT professionals believe their DNS infrastructure is not secure enough.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: HTMX 4.0: Hypermedia finds a new gear
item.description: HTMX has been considered feature-complete for some time. It is a successful project that achieves its ambitious goals and is widely hailed, not to mention widely deployed in production. HTMX 2.0 was considered the final word. The creator promised there would be no HTMX 3.0.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Curious about quantum? Check out training options from ISC2, IBM, AWS and more
item.description: Announcements from quantum computing companies continue to shorten the time before we reach Q-day. That’s the day when quantum computers get powerful enough to use for general business applications—or to break existing encryption standards.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Linux 7.0 debuts with some big changes for networking
item.description: The Linux 7.0 kernel is now out, and it’s one of the most impactful releases in years for networking professionals. The Linux kernel is the core of a Linux operating system distribution. Linux is commonly used as a foundation for operating systems in the cloud and as a base for networking.
item.dc:creator: Network World
